question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted ATOMIC_BATCH transaction whose inner transaction list references a `payerAccountId` and `validStartNs` pair belonging to a completely unrelated third-party transaction, causing that third-party transaction to appear as a child of the attacker's batch in the mirror node's history view, thereby reorganizing the visible transaction order for that account?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user craft an ATOMIC_BATCH transaction where `innerTransactions` (List<Long>) is populated with duplicate `payerAccountId`/`validStartNs` pairs, causing the same inner transaction to appear multiple times in the mirror node's history, reorganizing the apparent execution order and count of that transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user submit an ATOMIC_BATCH where `addInnerTransaction()` only validates `this.type != TransactionType.ATOMIC_BATCH.getProtoId()` but does NOT validate that the inner transaction's `parentConsensusTimestamp` actually matches the batch's `consensusTimestamp`, allowing an attacker to inject an unrelated transaction into a batch's `innerTransactions` list and reorder its history position?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addInnerTransaction()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `addInnerTransaction()` appends `transaction.getPayerAccountId().getId()` and `transaction.getValidStartNs()` without verifying the inner transaction's `batchKey` matches the outer batch's `batchKey`, allowing the attacker to associate arbitrary transactions with their batch and distort the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `isNew()` always returns `true` (line 139) to cause duplicate `Transaction` records with the same `consensusTimestamp` to be inserted without an upsert check, resulting in duplicate entries in the transaction history that distort the apparent ordering and count of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getId()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction whose `consensusTimestamp` collides with an existing transaction's `consensusTimestamp` (the @Id field), and because `isNew()` always returns `true` bypassing Hibernate's existence check, cause the new record to overwrite or shadow the existing transaction in the history, effectively deleting or replacing a prior transaction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a crafted `transactionHash` byte array that, when processed by `toTransactionHash()`, produces a `TransactionHash` record whose `hash` field collides with an existing transaction's hash, causing the mirror node to map two different `consensusTimestamp` values to the same hash and reorganize which transaction is returned for hash-based lookups?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: toTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where `transactionHash` is exactly 48 bytes, causing `TransactionHash.setHash()` to split it into a 32-byte `hash` and 16-byte `hashSuffix`, and by choosing the first 32 bytes to collide with a victim transaction's hash, cause the mirror node to return the attacker's transaction when the victim's hash is queried, reorganizing the apparent history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: parentConsensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a child transaction with a `parentConsensusTimestamp` pointing to a transaction they do not own, causing the mirror node to incorrectly link their transaction as a child of a victim's transaction, reorganizing the victim's transaction history to include the attacker's activity?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: parentConsensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction with a `parentConsensusTimestamp` that references a future `consensusTimestamp` not yet in the database, causing the mirror node's parent-resolution logic to fail silently and insert the transaction with a null or incorrect parent, disrupting the chronological ordering of the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: index)] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a manipulated `index` field value that conflicts with an existing transaction's index within the same block, causing the mirror node to present transactions in an incorrect order when sorted by index, reorganizing the apparent execution sequence?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: index)] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction with a negative or zero `index` value, causing it to sort before all legitimate transactions in index-ordered history queries, effectively prepending the attacker's transaction to the front of the block's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: nonce)] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with `nonce = 0` while also setting `batchKey` to a non-null value, causing `SqlEntityListener.onTransaction()` to attempt to look up a batch parent via `parentConsensusTimestamp`, and if the parent is found (belonging to another user), inject this transaction as an inner transaction of that batch, reorganizing the batch's inner transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: nonce)] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction with a non-zero `nonce` to bypass the `nonce == 0` check in `SqlEntityListener.onTransaction()`, preventing their transaction from being linked to a batch parent, and then submit a second transaction with `nonce = 0` referencing the same `parentConsensusTimestamp`, causing inconsistent parent-child linkage in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: errata)] Can an unprivileged user exploit any path that allows setting the `errata` field to `ErrataType.DELETE` on an existing transaction record, causing the mirror node to omit that transaction from history queries, effectively deleting a transaction from the visible history without direct fund theft?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: errata)] Can an unprivileged user exploit any path that allows setting the `errata` field to `ErrataType.INSERT` on a fabricated transaction record, causing the mirror node to insert a phantom transaction into the history that never actually occurred on the consensus network, reorganizing the apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: validStartNs)] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a `validStartNs` value set far in the past or future, causing the mirror node to store and display the transaction at an incorrect chronological position in history queries that sort or filter by `validStartNs`, reorganizing the apparent transaction order?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: validStartNs)] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where `validStartNs` matches the `validStartNs` of a victim's transaction with the same `payerAccountId`, causing the mirror node's deduplication or lookup logic to confuse the two transactions and return the attacker's transaction in place of the victim's, reorganizing the victim's history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that calls `addNftTransfer()` with an `NftTransfer` object referencing NFT serial numbers and account IDs belonging to a victim, causing the mirror node to record a phantom NFT transfer in the victim's transaction history, reorganizing the apparent NFT ownership history without actually moving funds?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addNftTransfer()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that triggers `addNftTransfer()` with a null-check bypass (the `@NonNull` annotation is only a compile-time hint), causing a NullPointerException or silent null insertion into the `nftTransfer` list that corrupts the transaction's NFT transfer history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that causes `addItemizedTransfer()` to be called with an `ItemizedTransfer` whose `entityId` references a victim account, inserting a phantom itemized transfer into the victim's transaction history and reorganizing the apparent transfer record?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: addItemizedTransfer()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction that causes `addItemizedTransfer()` to be called repeatedly with the same `ItemizedTransfer` object, causing duplicate entries in the `itemizedTransfer` JSON list stored in the database, reorganizing the apparent transfer amounts and counts in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: scheduled)] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with the `scheduled` flag set to `true` when the transaction was not actually scheduled, causing the mirror node to incorrectly categorize it as a scheduled transaction and reorganize it into the scheduled transaction history view, hiding it from the non-scheduled history?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: type)] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction with a `type` integer value that does not correspond to any known `TransactionType` enum entry (defaulting to `UNKNOWN(-1)`), causing the mirror node to misclassify the transaction type and reorganize it into an incorrect category in type-filtered history queries?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/Transaction.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: Transaction (field: type)] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction with `type` set to `TransactionType.ATOMIC_BATCH.getProtoId()` (74) when it is not actually an atomic batch, causing `SqlEntityListener.onTransaction()` to register it as a batch parent in the context map, and then have subsequent legitimate inner transactions incorrectly linked to it, reorganizing those transactions' parent-child history?
